question: A batch of material consists of 120 units where 40 units are oversized, 20 are undersized, and 60 conform to specification. Two units are drawn at random one after the other (without replacement). What is the probability that both units will be of the same category?
answer:
The probability of the event E that both units selected will be of the same category is

$$
P\{E\}= rac{13}{119}+ rac{19}{714}+ rac{59}{238}= rac{274}{714}=0.383
$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 1: Basic Probability, Statistics, and Reliability
